title: Panama Tax Residency: What Founders Need to Know
excerpt: Panama's territorial tax system, stable dollar economy, and Friendly Nations visa make it one of the most accessible offshore tax residencies for founders and remote workers.
html:
Panama taxes only income sourced within Panama. If your income comes from foreign clients, foreign investments, or a foreign entity, Panama will not tax it — regardless of how long you live there. For founders running international businesses, this is the core of the Panama tax proposition.

0%Tax on foreign income
3–6 moResidency timeline
$5,000Min. bank deposit (FNV)
USDOfficial currency

How does Panama's territorial tax system work?

Panama uses a strict source-based territorial system: income is taxable only if it originates from activities performed inside Panama. If you sell software to US customers, manage investments abroad, or operate a foreign LLC, that income is outside Panama's tax jurisdiction entirely. There is no controlled foreign corporation (CFC) regime and no exit tax.

Panama does have a corporate income tax for locally-sourced income, but founders operating through a foreign entity with no Panama-source revenue will generally owe nothing. Consult a local attorney to confirm your specific income streams qualify as foreign-sourced under Panamanian law.

What are the visa options for establishing residency?

The Friendly Nations Visa (FNV) is the most popular route for founders from qualifying countries (which includes most Western nations, the US, Canada, UK, Australia, and others). Requirements include a $5,000 deposit in a Panamanian bank account, proof of economic ties (typically a Panamanian corporation or employment contract), and a clean background check.

Processing takes 3–6 months and results in a permanent residency card after the temporary stage. Panama also offers a Qualified Investor Visa (minimum $300,000 in real estate, securities, or bank deposits) and a Self-Economic Solvency Visa for higher-net-worth applicants.

What's the banking situation in Panama?

Panama has a well-developed banking sector anchored in Panama City. Banks like Banistmo, Global Bank, and Multibank serve expats and non-residents with reasonable documentation requirements. The country uses the US dollar as its official currency, which eliminates currency risk and simplifies accounting for USD-earning founders.

Opening a personal account as a new resident typically requires your residency card, proof of income, and references. Correspondent banking relationships are solid, and international wire transfers are routine. Panama City's financial district is a functioning hub, not a tax haven pantomime.

How does Panama compare to other territorial jurisdictions?

Verdict

Panama is a serious, functioning tax residency option — not a novelty. The dollar economy, accessible Friendly Nations Visa, and genuinely territorial tax system make it one of the best-value options for founders who want legal tax optimization without relocating to a desert city or a post-Soviet capital. If you're already doing business in USD and want a Latin American base, Panama belongs at the top of your list.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need to spend a minimum number of days in Panama to maintain residency?

Panama does not impose a formal minimum stay requirement for permanent residents in practice, though you are technically required to enter Panama at least once every two years to maintain status. Tax residency certificates are available for those who do spend significant time there.

Can US citizens use Panama to escape US taxation?

No. US citizens are taxed on worldwide income regardless of residency. Panama's territorial system only helps non-US founders or US citizens who have renounced US citizenship or long-term permanent residence.

Is Panama on any tax haven blacklists?

Panama has had EU and OECD compliance issues in the past but has substantially updated its legal framework. Check current EU and OECD grey/blacklist status before making decisions, as this changes periodically.

What is the cost of living in Panama City?

Panama City is moderately priced by Latin American standards — comparable to a mid-tier European city. Quality neighborhoods like Punta Pacifica and El Cangrejo have good international restaurants, healthcare, and infrastructure.
